dependsOn.guidance: Briefs that must be built before this one — usually the tables this model's relationships point at.

instructions.whoFillsThisIn: Developers (Product can sanity-check the information being stored).
instructions.when: Whenever a page needs to store data. One of these per model/table.
instructions.how: Answer in plain English where you can.
instructions.note: Solution = the folder this file sits in. Table / object name = this file's name.

whatThisModelIs.question: What does this model represent, in one line?
whatThisModelIs.answer: A unit of planned work on a Product's planning board — an epic, feature, user story, task, bug, or test — arranged in a hierarchy, optionally assigned to a team member and scheduled into a sprint or dates.

fields.guidance: One entry per piece of data.

entries.description: Short title of the work item

entries.rules: one of: epic / feature / userStory / task / bug / test
entries.description: What kind of work item this is. epic/feature/userStory/task form the planning hierarchy (which levels exist comes from the planningHierarchy setting); bug and test may attach at any level

entries.rules: one of: planned / designing / building / testing / done
entries.description: Where the item is in the workflow
The Product this work belongs to

entries.rules: must reference a WorkItem row when set; a hierarchy child's type must sit deeper in the active planning hierarchy than its parent's type (bug/test attach anywhere)
must reference a TeamMember row when set
entries.description: The team member the work is assigned to

entries.rules: must reference a Sprint row of the same Product when set
entries.description: The sprint the item is scheduled into
Optional start date — teams that don't set times leave this empty

entries.rules: must not be before startDate when both are set

relationships.guidance: How this model connects to others.

lookupsAndPerformance.question: Which fields do we search or filter on a lot?

retention.question: How long do we keep this data / when is it deleted?
retention.answer: Until the user deletes the item or its Product. Removing a team member or sprint clears the reference — never deletes the item.

sensitiveFields.question: Any sensitive fields that need extra protection?

invariants.question: Anything that must always be true?
invariants.answer: A work item always has a title, a type, and a Product. A hierarchy child sits deeper in the active planning hierarchy than its parent (bug/test attach anywhere). A work item with no WorkItemPolicy row is completely closed to AI (deny-by-default). Dates are optional; when both are set, endDate is not before startDate.

changesOverTime.guidance: Each time you come back to change this model, add an entry describing what should change.

rounds.change: Planning now attaches to Products, not repositories: productId replaces repositoryId. itemType list becomes epic/feature/userStory/task (the hierarchy, governed by the planningHierarchy setting) plus bug/test (any level). New optional fields: assigneeId (TeamMember), sprintId (Sprint), startDate, endDate — all nullable so teams that don't assign or schedule aren't forced to.

Add a Deliverable link and commercial fields: deliverableId (nullable FK → Deliverable of the same Product), expectedCost, estimatedProfit (nullable money), chargeable (yes/no, default no), customerCoverPct (nullable 0–100). The three commercial fields' visibility is gated per Role in the UI (Role.seeCost/seeProfit/seeChargeable).
